text: {"fixed_xml":"<ITEM>In dem Bericht-Hau√ü ist ein Project zu sehen eines Theolo. gischen Tractats unter dem Titul: <BIBL><TITLE>Geschrey um Mitternacht, siehel der Br√§utigam kommt: Gehet hinau√ü ihme entgegen.</TITLE> Oder, <VOLUME>38.</VOLUME> Predigten von dem j√ºngsten Gericht und der darauf folgenden seeligen und √ºnseligen Ewig. keit, nebst einem zweyfachen Andang; Erstlich, von <VOLUME>4.</VOLUME> Bu√ü. Predigten. Zweytens: Einer Jahr Aus- und Jahr-Eingangs-Predigt, uc.</BIBL> welcher Praenumarations- weise in <PLACE>St. Gallen</PLACE> gedrucket werden solle; die Herren Praenumeranten aber haben keine Zeit zu versaumen, inmassen, zufolg diesem Project, die Praenumerations-Gelder nicht l√§nger als bi√ü Eingangs <YEAR>Septembris</YEAR> angenohmen werden. Man zahlet zum voraus <PRIZE>45. xr.</PRIZE> und bey Empfang des gantzen Werckes annoch <PRIZE>45. xr.</PRIZE> das weitere kan aus dem Project verstanden werden.</ITEM>","number_of_corrections":2,"explanation":"Two corrections were made: 1) Removed the erroneous second opening `<TITLE>` tag that appeared after \"uc.\" — the text after the first `</TITLE>` tag (starting with \"Oder, ...\") is a continuation of the bibliographic description but was incorrectly wrapped in a second `<TITLE>` tag. The closing `</TITLE>` before `</BIBL>` was replaced with the correct `</BIBL>` closing tag. Specifically, the original had a nested/duplicate `<TITLE>...</TITLE>` structure that was malformed: the second `</TITLE>` before `</BIBL>` was extraneous since there was no corresponding second `<TITLE>` opening tag. The fix removes the spurious `</TITLE>` that closed a tag never opened, keeping the `</BIBL>` to properly close the bibliographic reference. 2) The original XML had `</TITLE></BIBL>` at the end of the bibliographic section, but only one `<TITLE>` was opened. The second `</TITLE>` was removed, leaving just `</BIBL>` to properly close the `<BIBL>` element."}

parsed.explanation: Two corrections were made: 1) Removed the erroneous second opening `<TITLE>` tag that appeared after "uc." — the text after the first `</TITLE>` tag (starting with "Oder, ...") is a continuation of the bibliographic description but was incorrectly wrapped in a second `<TITLE>` tag. The closing `</TITLE>` before `</BIBL>` was replaced with the correct `</BIBL>` closing tag. Specifically, the original had a nested/duplicate `<TITLE>...</TITLE>` structure that was malformed: the second `</TITLE>` before `</BIBL>` was extraneous since there was no corresponding second `<TITLE>` opening tag. The fix removes the spurious `</TITLE>` that closed a tag never opened, keeping the `</BIBL>` to properly close the bibliographic reference. 2) The original XML had `</TITLE></BIBL>` at the end of the bibliographic section, but only one `<TITLE>` was opened. The second `</TITLE>` was removed, leaving just `</BIBL>` to properly close the `<BIBL>` element.
